feed.title: Accessibility Wars - mesmer
feed.subtitle: Accessible Builds for the Guild Wars 2 Community

entry.title: Rifle and Roses [qHeal]
entry.content:
The Troubadour has an easy design such that when you randomly mash all the buttons, you'd provide all the right boons and heals. This build has selected utilities for minimal cognitive complexity, such that the hardest part of playing this build is enduring the out-of-sync music throughout the fight.

While this guide assumes no weapon swap is needed, if you take an off-tank role, you may consider swapping rifle to other alternatives.

Gearing and Consumables

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Rifle Harrier's Paralyzation
Transference
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Harrier's
Amulet Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Harrier's
Ring Harrier's
Relic Febe

This is a low toughness set-up.

Alternative

Weapon swap is optional. Consider scepter and shield for tanking and focus for additional CC/pulls.

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Scepter Harrier's Transference
Shield Harrier's Paralyzation
Focus Minstrel's Renewal

Shield is better used for tanking and focus is useful for pulling adds. Alternate weapon sets should be prioritised based on utility requirements.

 

Budget Options

  • Replace any of the sigil with  Superior Sigil of Water

Consumables

  •  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish OR  Delicious Rice Ball (budget alternative)
  •  Magnanimous Maintenance Oil OR  Holographic Super Drumstick (budget alternative)

Build

Heal Quickness Build

[&DQctOhcqSTptAQ8BUx2GAYQdgQFrHWsBbR28AQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAABWQAA]

Rotation and Utilities

There is no fixed rotation for this build. Even if you randomly press buttons you will still provide boons and adequate heals.

Quickness

You will need to use the following for quickness:

  1. Lively Lute
  2. Crescendo (while as many instruments are playing as possible)

Swiftness

  1. Mantra of Recovery
  2. Power Return

Your healing skills trigger  Relic of Febe to grant swiftness, use them off cooldown.

Shield or Focus can partially cover quickness, allowing you to drop  Relic of Febe

General rotation

  1. Generate Notes (Clones)
    • Journey (R2)
    • Phantasmal Sharpshooter (R4)
    • Mantra of Recovery -> Power Return (Heal)
    • Any tale with instrument-pairing
  2. Spend Notes to play Instruments as soon as they recharged (2 or more notes)
  3. Use Crescendo when you have 3 or more instruments playing
  4. You provide lots of boons and healing through instruments, rifle skills and utilities provide even more of them.

Utilty

You may replace skill slot 9 with any of the following:

  1. Tale of the Valiant Marshal for a stun break + short stability + additional notes
  2. Mantra of Concentration for permanent stability
  3. Mantra of Resolve for heavy condi cleanses
  4. Feedback for reflects
  5. Portal Entre for skips

Crowd Control

You have decent CC with your base kit:

  1. Phantasmal Sharpshooter (R4)
  2. Deafening Drum (F3)
  3. Tale of the August Queen (Elite)
  4. Crescendo (while Drum is playing)

Your weapon swaps allow access to additional CCs.

Optimisations

Mesmer as a class is designed to be flexible. The skill choices on this site are designed to be simple. There are an incredible amount of permutations that are not exhausted in this guide. You may introduce additional utilities when you are comfortable so play around.

entry.title: Dune Cloak [aDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

The concept behind Dune Cloak Alacrity Condition Mirage is that you want to use an Ambush attack, specifically Chaos Vortex, as much as possible, and how you get an Ambush attack is by dodging, or Shattering with 3 clones. Your next auto attack will be an Ambush after dodging or Shattering. This is especially important as it is designed to be a support build, and you will give Alacrity through your Ambush attacks.

Ambush attacks also provide high uptime of both Might and Fury for your subgroup.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Staff Viper's Bursting
Torment
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Viper's
Amulet Viper's
Accessory Viper's
Accessory Viper's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Viper's
Ring Viper's
Relic Fractal

If you are having difficulty maintaining Alacrity uptime, replace Sigil of Bursting with Sigil of Concentration for a small DPS loss.

  • Food:  Meaty Asparagus Skewer
  • Enhancement:  Toxic Tuning Crystal

Build

[&DQctPRg9OzsjDwAAggEAAIEBAACDAQAA7RIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADWQBWAGYAAA==]

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3)
  2. Dodge --> Chaos Vortex (Ambush)
  3. Chaos Armor (Staff 4)
  4. Chaos Storm (Staff 5)

Your highest priority skill is Chaos Vortex (Ambush). To use Chaos Vortex (Ambush), you will either have to dodge or use a Shatter with 3 clones to gain Mirage Cloak.

Skill Priority:

  1. Dodge and use Chaos Vortex without letting your energy fill up completely
  2. Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3) --> Signet of the Ether --> Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3)
  3. Chaos Armor (Staff 4)
  4. Chaos Storm (Staff 5)

On top of this, you want to use a Shatter skill whenever you have 3 clones.

Shatter Priority:

  1. Cry of Frustration
  2. Mind Wrack

After using a Shatter, you will gain Mirage Cloak and automatically perform Chaos Vortex (Ambush). Do not use any other skills until you have finished casting Chaos Vortex (Ambush), as using any other skill will cancel Chaos Vortex (Ambush).

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority:

CC Skills:

  1. Signet of Humility
  2. Chaos Storm (Staff 5) (Delay this skill in your rotation if you know a CC bar is coming up)
  3. Signet of Midnight (Small CC, moderate DPS loss)
  4. Signet of Domination (Big CC, big DPS loss)

Advanced Tips

Distortion is also a very long immune skill, scaling up to a maximum of 3 seconds with the number of clones you currently have, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

Video

entry.title: Boon Power Chronomancer [Boon DPS]
entry.content:
Notes

Power Boon Chronomancer uses the exact same rotation concept as Power Chronomancer. By simply swapping 1 trait, we are able to provide either full Quickness OR Alacrity uptime to our subgroup, while also contributing decent damage.

It provides quickness/alacrity through the use of Phantasms or Shatter skills, both of which are already incorporated into the base Power Chronomancer build.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Assassin's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Eagle
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQcBHQoaKAYjDwAAjwEAAM8SAACCAQAAsxIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACWgAvAAA=]

Select Stretched Time if you want to give Alacrity, or Seize the Moment if you want to give Quickness.

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  4. Signet of the Ether
  5. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  6. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  7. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Signet of the Ether instantly recharges the cooldown of any Phantasmal type skill, and is used to deal more damage.

Use Well of Action off cooldown, as it activates Danger Time, giving you 10% more critical danage. Never delay Well of Action.

Your cooldowns will line up in such a way that you will always be able to pair Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) with either Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5) or Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) off cooldown, pairing it with the accompanying skill that is off cooldown.

Every few rotations, your cooldowns will line up such that you will be able to cast the following combo:

Burst Combo:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Signet of the Ether
  4. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  5. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  6. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Which is basically your opener minus the first Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use your burst combo as soon as it is available.

On top of this, you should also use Split Second off cooldown, after the first cast. You should wait until you have 3 clones before casting Split Second for the first time.

Split Second has no cast point, can be casted at the same time as other skills, and will not interrupt any other skill you are currently casting.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority

CC Skills:

  1. Gravity Well (BIG CC)
  2. Illusionary Leap > Swap (Sword 3)
  3. Time Sink (more clones more CC)

In most situations, just using Gravity Well would be enough contribution to break the CC bar.

Advanced Tips

Distortion is also a very long immune skill, scaling up to a maximum of 3 seconds with the number of clones you currently have, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

 

You can also block attacks with Illusionary Riposte (Sword 4), but it is not recommended unless you are very confident of the mechanic. An example of using this to increase DPS would be on Deimos, where its cooldown is short enough to block both the knockback "pizza" mechanic and mindcrush, allowing you to maximise damage uptime on the boss.

 

Video

entry.title: Power Troubadour [pDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

Melee Power build with high burst damage, ability to skip mechanics, and option to swap out utility for much more CC.

 

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Assassin's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
Berserker's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Assassin's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Eagle
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone
 

Build

[&DQcBHQoaSScjDwAAaQEAAIIBAABmHQAAbR0AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACWgAyAAA=]
 

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  2. Lively Lute
  3. Flustering Flute
  4. Deafening Drum
  5. Crescendo

Your opener is also your burst rotation, and should be used whenever Crescendo is coming off cooldown.

Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5) and Lively Lute have the same cooldown, and should always be used together. This is your highest priority and should not be delayed for anything else, and it should be extended into the burst rotation when Crescendo is available.

Take note that you do want to use Lively Lute with 3 Notes active for maximum damage, but by following this rotation you will always have 3 Notes when casting Lively Lute, removing one source of resource management.

On top of this, you should:

Use off cooldown:

  1. Power Spike (Only use 1 charge)
  2. Blurred Frenzy
  3. Tale of the Tortured Mastermind

In that order of priority. Power Spike has no cast time and can be used during the cast of another skill without interrupting it.

 

Tale of the August Queen is a 2 second long immune skill, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

 

You can block attacks with Illusionary Riposte (Sword 4), but it is not recommended unless you are very confident of the mechanic. An example of using this to increase DPS would be on Deimos, where it's cooldown is short enough to block both the knockback "pizza" mechanic and mindcrush, allowing you to maximise damage uptime on the boss.

 

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority

CC Skills:

  1. Illusionary Leap --> Swap
  2. Deafening Drum

and you can swap out Tale of the August Queen for Signet of Humility if a lot of CC is required for the encounter.

 

Video

entry.title: Condi Chronomancer [cDPS]
entry.content:
A strong condi dps option for the chronomancer. All staff, no swapping, heavy on the signets

Notes

Condition Chronomancer is a simple build with solid damage potential. Due to the high amounts of Confusion it applies, it excels at fights with fast-attacking bosses.

It has very high range and is able to provide all important Boons to itself in open world gameplay: (Might, Stability, Aegis, Regeneration, Quickness, Alacrity, Fury and Protection).

It can also be turned into a simple Alacrity or Quickness provider with just one trait change.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Staff Viper's Malice
Torment
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Viper's
Amulet Viper's
Accessory Viper's
Accessory Viper's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Viper's
Ring Viper's
Relic Fractal
  • Food:  Cilantro and Cured Meat Flatbread or  Plate of Beef Rendang
  • Enhancement:  Toxic Tuning Crystal

Build

[&DQcBJS09KD4jDwAAgwEAAIEBAABrAQAA7RIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Rotation

In the opener, we're making use of Continuum Split in a simple way to reset phantasms and creating a nice amount of burst damage.

Opener:

  1. Mirror Images -> Continuum Split
  2. Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3) -> Signet of the Ether -> Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3)
  3. Fit any other skill you are able to before Continuum Split ends
  4. Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3) -> Signet of the Ether -> Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3)

You can use this opener whenever there is a phase change and Continuum Split is off cooldown.

The rest of the rotation is priority based and should be performed after your opener.

Priority:

  1. Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3)
  2. Signet of the Ether -> Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3)
  3. Rewinder -> Split Second -> Mirror Images
  4. Chaos Armor (Staff 4)
  5. Chaos Storm (Staff 5)
  6. Phase Retreat (Staff 2)

Take note that Phase Retreat (Staff 2) teleports you away from your target by 360 distance, and you'll have to move back towards the target after every time you cast it.

Mirror Images has a longer cooldown than Rewinder and will not always be available. Skip Mirror Images if it is on cooldown.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority:

CC Skills:

  1. Signet of Humility
  2. Chaos Storm (Staff 5)
  3. Time Sink (Small DPS Loss)
  4. Signet of Domination (Big DPS Loss)

Signet of Humility does 600 breakbar damage, and will almost always be enough CC as long as everyone in your group is doing some CC. You should wait a bit before using your other CC skills once you have cast Signet of Humility.

Chaos Storm (Staff 5) is used in your damage rotation and might not always be available.

Build Concepts

Like core mesmer, the gameplay on chrono evolves around creating clones of yourself and then shattering them. Creating clones can be done in two ways:

  1. Directly by skills or traits that create clones on certain actions, like Phase Retreat (Staff 2)
  2. Indirectly by using phantasm skills, like Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3), which perform an attack and then turn into clones afterwards

Illusionary Membrane increases your condition damage by 7% for 5 seconds when you apply Chaos Aura to yourself and stacks in duration.

Sharper Images makes your illusions apply bleeding on critical hits, making it very important to have three clones up at all times. This build generates a lot of clones and rarely shatters them, making it relatively easy to execute.

Improved Alacrity makes Alacrity stronger but harder to keep up. With this trait, your skills recharge in ~67% (two thirds) of their original cooldown time. This increases the APM of the build, but is also a very strong boost to everything you do.

Chronophantasma makes your Phantasms exceptionally strong, since they do their damage twice in a row. That's why using  Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3) and resetting it with Signet of the Ether are your main priorities.

Continuum Split

Continuum Split deserves its own section, as it is one of the unique aspects of Chronomancer. It reverts time, in that it will save the skill cooldowns, health and position when you activate it. Then, for each Clone you shattered with it, it gives you more time to use skills and have them reverted.

Video

entry.title: Infinite Horizon [aDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

The concept behind Alacrity Condition Mirage is that you want to use an Ambush attack, specifically Chaos Vortex, as much as possible, and how you get an Ambush attack is by dodging, or picking up Mirage Mirrors. Your next auto attack will be an Ambush after dodging or picking up a Mirage Mirror. This is especially important as it is designed to be a support build, and you will give Alacrity through your Ambush attacks.

In this build Mirage Mirrors will be generated by False Oasis and Crystal Sands. False Oasis drops the Mirror where you're standing, but Crystal Sands drops the Mirror where you're aiming, and you want to always aim Crystal Sands inside the hitbox of the boss to ensure all of its hits land. You can use Jaunt, which is a shadowstep, to pick it up.

Ambush attacks also provide high uptime of both Might and Fury for your subgroup.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Viper's Nightmare
Viper's Nightmare
Viper's Nightmare
Viper's Nightmare
Viper's Nightmare
Viper's Nightmare
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Staff Viper's Bursting
Torment
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Viper's
Amulet Viper's
Accessory Viper's
Accessory Viper's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Viper's
Ring Viper's
Relic Fractal

If you are having difficulty maintaining Alacrity uptime, replace Sigil of Bursting with Sigil of Concentration for a small DPS loss.

  • Food:  Meaty Asparagus Skewer
  • Enhancement:  Toxic Tuning Crystal

Build

[&DQctPQElOxnuFQAA4BUAAIEBAACDAQAARhcAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADWQBWAGYAAA==]

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3)
  2. Dodge --> Chaos Vortex (Ambush)
  3. Crystal Sands
  4. Chaos Armor (Staff 4)
  5. Chaos Storm (Staff 5)
  6. False Oasis
  7. Dodge --> Chaos Vortex (Ambush)
  8. Pick up mirror from False Oasis --> Chaos Vortex (Ambush)
  9. Pick up mirror from Crystal Sands using Jaunt --> Chaos Vortex (Ambush)

Your highest priority skill is Chaos Vortex (Ambush). To use Chaos Vortex (Ambush), you will either have to dodge or to pick up a Mirror to gain Mirage Cloak.

Skill Priority:

  1. Crystal Sands (pick up mirror in boss hitbox using Jaunt)
  2. False Oasis (pick up mirror where you casted False Oasis)
  3. Dodge and use Chaos Vortex without letting your energy fill up completely
  4. Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3)
  5. Chaos Armor (Staff 4)
  6. Chaos Storm (Staff 5)

You do not need to pick up the mirage mirror created using Crystal Sands or False Oasis immediately, as they will last on the ground for 8 seconds before disappearing. This allows you to squeeze in other skills from your priority list, allowing for higher DPS.

Take care not to cancel Chaos Vortex (Ambush) by casting another skill while Chaos Vortex (Ambush) is casting.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority:

CC Skills:

  1. Chaos Storm (Staff 5) (Delay this skill in your rotation if you know a CC bar is coming up)
  2. Diversion (more clones more CC)
  3. Signet of Midnight (Small CC, moderate DPS loss)
  4. Signet of Domination (Big CC, big DPS loss)

If you CC'ed using Diversion, you want to generate more clones by casting Phantasmal Warlock (Staff 3) as soon as possible as much of your damage from this build comes from your clones. You can also use Phase Retreat (Staff 2) --> Jaunt to generate 2 clones and move back onto the boss as Phase Retreat (Staff 2) moves you away from the boss.

Advanced Tips

Distortion is also a very long immune skill, scaling up to a maximum of 3 seconds with the number of clones you currently have, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

Video

entry.title: Power Chronomancer [pDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

Power Chronomancer is primarily a combo based rotation, with one longer opener/skill combo and skill pairings.

It is able to do large amounts of CC due to the free elite skill slot, and has multiple utility skills that can be swapped around for a minor DPS loss to provide many utilities, such as reflecting, blinking, portalling, aegis, or stability.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Assassin's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Eagle
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQcBHQoaKDYjDwAAjwEAAM8SAACCAQAAsxIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACWgAvAAA=]

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  4. Signet of the Ether
  5. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  6. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  7. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Signet of the Ether instantly recharges the cooldown of any Phantasmal type skill, and is used to deal more damage.

Use Well of Action off cooldown, as it activates Danger Time, giving you 10% more critical danage. Never delay Well of Action.

Your cooldowns will line up in such a way that you will always be able to pair Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) with either Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5) or Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) off cooldown, pairing it with the accompanying skill that is off cooldown.

Every few rotations, your cooldowns will line up such that you will be able to cast the following combo:

Burst Combo:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Signet of the Ether
  4. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  5. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  6. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Which is basically your opener minus the first Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use your burst combo as soon as it is available.

On top of this, you should also use Split Second off cooldown, after the first cast. You should wait until you have 3 clones before casting Split Second for the first time.

Split Second has no cast point, can be casted at the same time as other skills, and will not interrupt any other skill you are currently casting.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority

CC Skills:

  1. Gravity Well (BIG CC)
  2. Illusionary Leap > Swap (Sword 3)
  3. Time Sink (more clones more CC)

In most situations, just using Gravity Well would be enough contribution to break the CC bar.

Advanced Tips

Distortion is also a very long immune skill, scaling up to a maximum of 3 seconds with the number of clones you currently have, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

 

You can also block attacks with Illusionary Riposte (Sword 4), but it is not recommended unless you are very confident of the mechanic. An example of using this to increase DPS would be on Deimos, where its cooldown is short enough to block both the knockback "pizza" mechanic and mindcrush, allowing you to maximise damage uptime on the boss.

 

Video

entry.title: Chrono Tank [aHeal/qHeal/Tank]
entry.content:
Assuming your party is experienced and only require a tank with multiple reliable blocks to ensure minimal risk of failure, majority of your actions will be based on the scepter auto-attack. The end of the auto-attack chain Ether Clone procs Illusionary Inspiration to provide heals and empower your shatter skills. Aegis and Stability are available on demand.

This build is has low physical complexity, has very few repetitions and is functional in instanced content, especially for new players wanting to volunteer for learning tank roles. You may find that the tanking ability is superior to other classes but relying on scepter alone without proper planning may result in mediocre healing. While this guide assumes no weapon swap is needed, to maximise its heal utility, you may consider swapping to rifle.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Scepter Minstrel's Concentration
Shield Minstrel's Transference
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Minstrel's
Amulet Minstrel's
Accessory Minstrel's
Accessory Minstrel's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Minstrel's
Ring Minstrel's
Relic Flock

Alternative

Consider rifle for additional healing and focus for additional CC/pulls.

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Rifle Minstrel's Concentration
Transference
Focus Minstrel's Transference

Note: Weapon swap to rifle is optional, but shield is better used for tanking. Alternate weapon sets should be prioritised based on utility requirements.

 

Budget Options

  • Replace  Superior Sigil of Transference with  Superior Sigil of Water

Consumables

  •  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish OR  Delicious Rice Ball (budget alternative)
  •  Magnanimous Maintenance Oil

Build

Heal Quickness Build

[&DQctOhcqKC9tAQ8BkxKGAc8SgQGPAWsBsxK8AQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Swap to stretched time for Alacrity instead of Quickness

Rotation and Utilities

Firstly, do not panic. Chronomancer is an easy but brainy class to play. A lot of mechanics are invisible and running in the backend.

Opener

You may wish to adapt an opener into your rotation for initial quickness. Time warp is also useful for initial quickness.

The methods of executing your opener is non-exhaustive and you will find new ways that will suit your own playstyle. Many opener variations may include utilities but generally fulfil the following purpose

  1. Generate Alacrity/Quickness with minimal downtime.
  2. Stack might right at the start of the fight
  3. Provide essential boons required for the particular encounter.
  4. Provide boons that are missing from the subgroup.

An example opener would be

  1. Continuum Split F5
  2. Tides of Time (Shield 5)
  3. F1-F4 All your shatter skills
  4. Tides of Time (Shield 5)

The above is used due to easy execution, instant alacrity/quickness and early might, fury, protection, resistance, stability, regeneration and vigor.

Principles of generating Alacrity/Quickness

  1. Generate Clones using autos - Ether Clone.
  2. Use shatter skills when clones are full to heal allies and grant alacrity/quickness.
  3. Using Phantasm skills from your weapon skills and utilties also generate a significant amount of alacrity/quickness.
  1. These three steps are the core foundation of your build.
  2. Your last attack on the auto chain generates clones so you won't have enough quickness if you don't finish the auto chains.
  3. The rotation for quickness and alacrity is the same.
  4. You need to reserve backup when you can't generate clones or give boons. That's what the utilities are for.
  5. Phantasms generate more alacrity than clones, it will be good to bring at least 1-2 from weapon skills or utilities.

Maintenance Heals

  1. Clone generation provides a small but steady heal.
  2. Shattering clones provides a more sizeable heal.

Burst Heals

  1. Reserve the use of your wells if you can anticipate a large attack (eg. VG greens)
  2. Use your Mantra of Recovery as your emergency heal.[1]
  3. Your shatter skills provide significant heals even without clones.[2]
  4. Additional Mantras can be used in place of wells for very mobile fights.

Crowd Control

  1. You have decent CC by just using Tides of Time (Shield 5).
  2. Diversion (Shatter F3) provides a small CC.
  3. Signet of Humility (Elite) for a mega CC.
  4. Gravity Well (Elite) for a sizeable CC with other beneficial effects.
  5. Continuum Split can double cast some of your CC skills.

Tanking Rotation

  1. Cycle between your blocks when receiving heavy attacks
    • Illusionary Counter
    • Echo of Memory -> Deja Vu
    • Well of Precognition
  2. Other sources of group aegis can also be used but reserve it when it is absolutely required.

Other utilities and trait combos

  1. Bring Phantasmal Disenchanter / Arcane Thievery for boon strips.
  2. You may take Master of Manipulation for more/backup group aegis.
  3. Advanced strategies can be used with Mimic to duplicate skills for example:
    • Multi-feedback
    • Multi-blink
  4. Advanced strategies can be used with Continuum Split to duplicate skills for example:
    • Double Conjured Slash
    • Double Moa CC
  5. More advanced strategies can be used by combining Mimic and Continuum Split

Optimisation

Raid Ready

  1. W1 Vale Guardian - Use wells just before greens. This allows heals to come in right after green triggers. You can also use Well of Precognition to shield allies against teleport circles. Phantasmal Disenchanter and Arcane Thievery can strip boons off the Blue Guardian.
  2. W1 Gorseval - Start with initial stability which protects against the initial swipe. Subsequently, face the boss away from your group. Mantra of Concentration is also useful here.
  3. W1 Sabetha - You can teleport through flame wall with Blink. You have lots of CC options for Knuckles.
  4. W2 Slothasor - You need steady condition cleanses so spread the use of your shatters. Swapping to rifle is useful to heal players dropping poisons who are out of reach, bonus points if you can port them back to the group (coordination is difficult). Into the Void is often used to deal with slublings.
  5. W2 Matthias - Bring Feedback for reflects but if you are short of them, you can use Mimic to reset them. You have lots of CC options, but you may want to preserve Signet of Humility (Elite) when sacrifice mechanic is about to fail. The focus reflect options are too clunky to be reliable on this fight.
  6. W3 Escort - Most Chrono Tanks can swap to become a Tower Chrono. This is a niche build and not covered in this guide.
  7. W3 Twisted Castle - You may wish to attempt the button with the statues (left split). In this case after the double doors, use Echo of Memory immediately after entering and face them as they will throw hammers, then Blink to the next button. More skips can be done with an elementalist pair combo using Fiery Greatsword and clever use of portals and blink.
  8. W3 Xera - You are likely to tank this fight. This fight is mostly based on positioning. Into the Void (Focus Pull 4) is useful when tanking as it allows your team to cleave them more easily.
  9. W4 Cairn - Stability is required for skipping greens. Bring Well of Precognition and Mantra of Concentration.
  10. W4 Mursaat Overseer - Improved alacrity will allow you to claim tiles faster than other class. Using Continuum Split to duplicate Claim will allow you to claim tiles even faster.
  11. W4 Samarog - Try to bait as tank as much as possible. You can pre-cast your CC such as Tides of Time (Shield 5) before he pins you down (health bar indicator).
  12. W4 Deimos - Group Aegis is useful on CM. I prefer to Group Aegis before casting a Channeled block. You can also use manipulations for group aegis.
  13. W5 Soulless Horror - You will be tanking this fight and would definitely appreciate the added toughness. Bring Signet of Humility for this fight for the additional CC. Continuum Split has killed many Chrono Tanks in this fight so spatial awareness is important.
  14. W5 Dhuum - You will be tanking this fight and may need to do greens. If you do greens, Blink will be useful.
  15. W6 Conjured Amalgamate - You can CC multiple shields with Tides of Time (Shield 5) and Gravity Well. Conjured Slash can be duplicated with Continuum Split but you must click fast.
  16. W6 Twin Largos - This is a very mobile and difficult fight and you can mis-time your wells. However, Chronomancer is one of the best classes to take on either lanes.
  17. W6 Qadim - You have lots of CC options, some of which can be pre-casted for the mini bosses. You will need to tank one of the wyverns. Most of the time you will be bringing Portal Entre for the split phase and jumping puzzle.
  18. W7 Sabir - With clever use of Continuum Split, you can generate enough CC to break the bar during the 'coordinated' phase.
  19. W7 Adina - Tank on the grass. Just alternating your three blocks is enough. Feedback can be duplicated with Continuum Split and Mimic. For most groups, a 4th projectile block is not required but in case your options are exhausted, Phantasmal Warden. Temporal Curtain (Focus 4) may fail to block projectiles due to height limitations and you will need to sacrifice a trait.
  20. W7 QtP - You will be tanking this fight. Your timely channelled blocks are extrely useful to bypass attacks.
  21. W8 Greer - A reflection variant of this build makes this fight much easier.

  1. You can overheal using  Relic of the Flock. This generates barrier but has an ICD so you want to spread your casts. ↩

  2. Using all your shatters in panic without clones will destroy your boon uptime. ↩

entry.title: Flying Blades [cDPS]
entry.content:
A primarily bleed based build that applies bleed through critical hits.

Notes

Condition Virtuoso is a build that is simple to play and provides very consistent damage with high range. That allows you to freely position yourself and maintain your damage output, even when mechanics force you into range.

This build is also capable of doing large amounts of CC, and has multiple skills that can be swapped around for more utility, making it an excellent build for almost all bosses and situations.

It is also relatively sturdy for a DPS build, and has good HP self sustain.

Gearing

There are multiple gear combinations that will arrive at the same total value of stats.

ArmorStatUpgrade
Sinister Krait
Sinister Krait
Rampager's Krait
Rampager's Krait
Sinister Krait
Rampager's Krait
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Dagger Viper's Bursting
Torch Viper's Earth
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Rampager's
Amulet Rampager's
Accessory Rampager's
Accessory Sinister
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Rampager's
Ring Rampager's
Relic Fractal
  • Food:  Cilantro and Cured Meat Flatbread or  Plate of Beef Rendang
  • Enhancement:  Toxic Tuning Crystal

Build

[&DQctPRg9Qj9tAW4BggEAAIMBAACBAQAA5RoAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADLwAxAFoAAA==]

Rotation

To maximise damage on Condition Virtuoso, you should space out your Bladesong Skills in order to upkeep Deadly Blades, as this provides you with a +5% all damage buff. However, as you only want to use Bladesong Skills when you have 5 stacks of Blades, this naturally spaces out your Bladesong Skills, allowing you to maintain full uptime of Deadly Blades.

Opener:

  1. Bladesong Sorrow
  2. Phantasmal Mage (Torch 5)
  3. Bladecall (Dagger 2)
  4. Unstable Bladestorm (Dagger 3)
  5. Wait for 5 stacks of Blades
  6. Bladeturn Requiem
  7. Signet of Illusions

The rest of your rotation follows a priority list of skills you should use off cooldown.

Use Off Cooldown:

  1. Bladesong Sorrow (only use with 5 blades)
  2. Bladesong Harmony (only use with 5 blades)
  3. Bladeturn Requiem (only use with 5 blades)
  4. Phantasmal Mage (Torch 5)
  5. Unstable Bladestorm (Dagger 3)
  6. Bladecall (Dagger 2)
  7. The Prestige (Torch 4)

Outside of this priority, you should also cast Thousand Cuts as soon as it is off cooldown, as it has no cast time and will not interrupt your current action.

After every cast of Bladeturn Requiem, if Signet of Illusions is off cooldown, you should use it immediately, as in the opener, pairing these 2 skills together whenever possible.

You should also pair Mantra of Recovery with The Prestige (Torch 4), casting it whenever you have used The Prestige (Torch 4). Mantra of Recovery places an Ethereal Field every other cast due to Method of Madness and The Prestige (Torch 4) is a Blast finisher with a 3 second delay. The two skills share the same cooldown, so you can always pair them.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority

CC Skills:

  1. Phantasmal Mage (Torch 5)
  2. Bladesong Dissonance (use with as many blades as possible)
  3. Signet of Domination (big DPS loss, not recommended)

As Phantasmal Mage (Torch 5) is used in your damage rotation, it might not always be off cooldown. You should delay it if you know a CC bar is about to come up.

If more CC is required, replace Thousand Cuts with Signet of Humility. Signet of Humility is one of the largest CC skills in the game, doing 600 breakbar damage, and should be prioritised above all other CC skills if you have brought it. Skip the rest of your CC skills if you have used Signet of Humility, as using those CC skills is a DPS loss.

Blades and Related Traits

  • Blade skills deserve their own section, as it is one of the unique aspects of Virtuoso.
  • They replace Clones and related traits 1-to-1, with the exception that they are not linked to the target they are used against. Every Blade stocked remains above you as a floating dagger, empowering your Shatter skills.
  • You stock one Blade every 10 seconds when out of combat.
  • Through Bloodsong, you will stock a blade on every fifth stack of Bleeding you apply.
  • Paired with Jagged Mind (and Sharper Images on Dueling), you will stock up Blades very fast. This relies on Critical hits, making Precision and other sources of Critical chance an important stat on Virtuoso.

Advanced Tips

Bladesong Distortion is also a very long immune skill, up to 3.75 seconds if you use it with maximum stacks of blades, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

 

If you are fighting a boss that requires Reflect (i.e Matthias, Greer),

Replace:

  1. Signet of Midnight

With:

  1. Feedback

And use Feedback whenever you need to reflect.

Video
